title: acer saccharophorum var. rugelii
intro: Acer saccharophorum var. rugelii, also known as the Rugel's maple, is a deciduous tree or shrub species within the Acer saccharophorum complex. The bark and roots of this plant contain a compound called berberine, which has been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Acer saccharophorum var. rugelii include its potential to help reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function, among other benefits. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
